text: Why Does ICE Need OKX in a World Without a Closing Bell? OKXICE is not simply about OKX entering traditional finance, but about ICE confronting a market without a closing bell. Traditional clearing, settlement, collateral, regulation, and customer asset protection were built around fixed trading hours and banking days, while crypto operates continuously through stablecoins, wallets, global users, and real-time risk management. ICE brings licenses, clearing infrastructure, institutional trust, and formal risk controls; OKX brings 24/7 liquidity, users, leverage, and crypto-native account systems. The joint venture will test whether traditional and digital assets can function within a regulated, always-on collateral and margin system, particularly during weekends, holidays, and extreme market shocks when banks are closed. — link

text: Vietnam to Fine Users Up to $1,900 for Trading on Unlicensed Crypto Platforms Vietnam has issued Decree No. 284/2026/ND-CP, introducing administrative penalties for crypto-related violations ahead of the launch of its regulated digital asset market. Under the new rules, investors trading via unlicensed crypto platforms may face fines of up to VND 50 million (around $1,900), while unauthorized token offerings and serious AML violations may incur penalties of up to VND 200 million ($7,700). The decree will take effect on Sept. 1. Vietnam ranked fourth globally in Chainalysis' 2025 Global Crypto Adoption Index. — link
author: admin#1534592031

text: Brian Armstrong: Social Media Posts Are Not Token Endorsements or Investment Signals Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong said posts, profile-picture changes and memes shared from his personal X account should not be treated as endorsements of tokens or projects, noting that he may not know when an image has been linked to a token. Addressing criticism over support for the Base ecosystem, Armstrong said Coinbase and Base will continue backing builders through accelerators, grants, venture investments and product integrations, but will not promote specific tokens or push for listings. Crypto KOL @RuneCrypto_ questioned Coinbase co-founder and CEO Brian Armstrong's claim that he may not have known his posts were associated with a token. @RuneCrypto_ said BRIAN, a Base token named after Armstrong, generated more than $100 million in trading volume and attracted over 40,000 traders, while thousands of users tagged Armstrong and asked him to respond. He argued that whether Armstrong knew about the token and later claimed otherwise, or genuinely failed to notice it, either possibility pointed to a disconnect between Coinbase leadership and Base users. — link

text: Falcon Finance Launches Falcon Card, Enabling Spending via Tokenized Stocks and Gold Collateral Falcon Finance has introduced Falcon Card, a payment card that allows eligible users to spend USDf for everyday purchases online and offline. Users can mint the overcollateralized synthetic dollar USDf by depositing supported collateral assets or acquire USDf on secondary markets before transferring it to Falcon Card for spending. Supported collateral assets include stablecoins, major cryptocurrencies, tokenized gold such as XAUt, Ondo tokenized stocks including COINon, QBTSon, NVDAon, MSTRon and TSLAon, as well as xStocks assets such as MSTRX, SPYx, CRCLX, NVDAX and TSLAX. Falcon said the card is available to verified users across more than 90 jurisdictions. Sponsored by Falcon Finance — link

text: Russia's State Duma to Hold Final Readings on Crypto Regulation Bill According to TASS, Russia's State Duma will consider a cryptocurrency regulation bill in its second and third readings on July 21. The proposal would establish a legal framework for crypto activity, strengthen enforcement against illicit use and support authorized use in international transactions. It would also allow non-qualified investors to buy selected liquid cryptocurrencies after passing a test, subject to an annual limit of RUB 300,000 per intermediary. — link

text: Onchain reacting as expected the past 24 hours. Slow grind up on low volume and people positioning in the leaders again. I don't have a great read on majors or macro since I've been spending all my time onchain. Only pillar I'm willing to sit on is the fact that MSTR and STRC isn't an issue and fud related to has minimal impact on the short and medium term, if it was to have long term impact then the discussion wouldn't be on MSTR but something in the traditional system that is broken. RH eco seems more separated from majors performance whereas Ansem and Sol coins feel much more correlated. Only coins I'd be buying on Sol are Ansem and TripleT for longer term plays, everything else doesn't have enough mindshare to survive. Since Vlad's given so few hints as to what they value and want to support, traders are only buying coins which reflect potential follows from him or the RH team. Dividend yield and accumulating RWA assets through them is somewhat interesting. If you're taking a Pendle model approach then the security concerns to low liq or synthetic oracle pricing is the biggest concern. The idea is cool but pulling off that product needs some serious brain power. Not a fan of ponzies with stock/rwa pay outs and obviously will die as soon as something of higher quality comes along. You can almost look at these as the next layer in the pyramid above memes when markets start reversing. Blends a mix of utility and degeneracy for those with a maths and memic edge. Traders play these narratives as Vlad/team hasn't given any other clues besides a couple tweets on rwa's. Agentic trading, Ai inference and potentially some memory/hardware style plays are much more interesting for myself. This is the third narrative which RH are clearly backing, haven't spoken about and will likely be the biggest driver of volume and revenue to their business over the coming 12 months imo. Instead of people trading manually, I can see how it evolves where traders become more accustom to finding what their edge is and retail users eventually taking a more quantitative approach as markets will only get harder and more volatile. If each person has their own agent thats taking significantly more trades each day then that'll equate to more revenue, also the fact that they can potentially bring in non U.S citizens in the future through the agents mcp Cashcat looking strong and I'd be excited if we break through 90-100 mil, no I don't think teddies will vamp and if it did it wouldn't be beneficial for the chain as it'll show people with conviction they can't hold the leading assets as rotations are too violent. Say what you want but incentivising new pairs and vamping leaders will not help the chain.

text: Two-Thirds of Polymarket World Cup Traders Lost Money According to @defioasis, nearly 130,000 of more than 194,000 unique addresses trading Polymarket's World Cup winner market recorded losses, representing 66.7% of participants. Most lost less than $100, while 43 addresses lost more than $100,000 each, totaling over $15 million. Profits were similarly concentrated, with 54 addresses earning more than $100,000 each and collectively making over $22 million. — link

text: Crypto Payments to Gray-Market Peptide Vendors Reach $32M in Q1 According to Bloomberg, U.S. consumers are increasingly using cryptocurrency to buy gray-market peptides after banks and card networks restricted payments to some suppliers. Chainalysis data shows vendors received about $32 million in crypto in Q1 2026, up 159% from the previous quarter and putting the market on pace to exceed $100 million annually. Some China-based suppliers accept only bitcoin, prompting first-time buyers to open Coinbase accounts. — link

text: Former New York Governor Andrew Cuomo Joins OKX Board OKX announced that former New York Governor Andrew M. Cuomo has joined its board of directors. Cuomo, who previously served as New York attorney general and U.S. secretary of housing and urban development, has advised OKX on U.S. regulatory and institutional strategy since 2023. OKX said his appointment will support its U.S. expansion and Cuomo will also serve as co-chair of the joint venture between OKX and NYSE parent ICE. — link

text: QCP Market Colour, 20 July 2026 Markets are firmly risk-off, with geopolitics adding another layer of pressure. Oil is supported, equities are under strain, and BTC is holding a narrow $63,000 to $65,000 range. US equities ended the week lower, led by semiconductors as investors grew concerned that hyperscalers could moderate AI infrastructure spending. Capital rotated toward defensives and energy, with Brent above $85 and on course for a weekly gain of more than 10% after the US reimposed a naval blockade near the Strait of Hormuz. Crypto is caught in the same two-sided setup. BTC is consolidating near $64,100 after dipping below $63,000, while flows have turned supportive, with US spot ETFs recording four straight days of inflows after a record $8 billion outflow streak. Volatility stays offered, leaving front-end options inexpensive against the oil tail risk into month-end. With Brent bid and the Fed leaning hawkish into the 28 to 29 July FOMC, which side of the range breaks first? Read the full Market Colour here.

text: Zilliqa Reports Theft of ZIL From Exchange Partner's Cold Wallet Zilliqa said that one of its exchange partners suffered a security incident in which ZIL was stolen from a cold wallet. The incident is under active investigation, and the team is working with relevant parties to determine the root cause and full scope. As a precaution, all exchanges have been notified and asked to temporarily pause ZIL deposits and withdrawals to prevent the stolen funds from being moved or sold through centralized platforms. The affected exchange and the amount stolen have not yet been disclosed. Zilliqa is a blockchain network launched in 2017 and was among the earliest public blockchains to implement sharding technology. — link

text: Grayscale Plans Quarterly Cash Payouts From ETH and SOL Staking Rewards According to CryptoSlate, Grayscale plans to amend the trust agreements for its Ethereum Staking ETF (ETHE) and Solana Staking ETF (GSOL), allowing ETH and SOL staking rewards to be converted into cash and distributed to shareholders at least quarterly after fees. The changes could take effect as early as August 7, with payment dates and amounts depending on staking rewards, expenses and tax considerations. ETHE previously converted staking rewards accrued between October 6 and December 31, 2025 into cash and distributed about $9.39 million, or approximately $0.083 per share. — link

text: Circle President Heath Tarbert Sold About $30.8 Million of CRCL Shares Since June 2025 SEC Form 4 filings show that Circle President and former CFTC Chair Heath Tarbert has sold CRCL shares 10 times since June 2025, totaling about $30.77 million. The transactions mainly involved stock sales and option exercises. He still holds roughly 503,000 shares, with no disclosed open-market purchases. Tarbert previously told FOX Business, when asked about CRCL falling from around $260 to $62, that Circle remains focused on the long term and that if it succeeds in building financial infrastructure for the internet, "the stock will take care of itself." — link

text: Highlight Clip Jeff Yan: I'm not interested in imitation, only in zero-to-one innovation Jeff Yan: I'm not interested in imitation, only in zero-to-one innovation On July 9, 2026, in an interview on the VALRdotcom podcast Hyperliquid co-founder Jeff Yan stated that the team is trying to build mostly things that do not yet exist to achieve a zero-to-one leap. He believes that building something merely for feature parity because 10 people have already done it is not exciting, nor is it something the team would devote all their minds to. Citing HIP-3 as an example, Yan noted that this pioneering permissionless deployment structure creates an interesting synergy among protocols, deployers, interfaces, and exchanges. He expressed that building things that do not exist yet cannot be informed by metrics; metric growth is simply a downstream benefit, and the right approach is to always keep finding the next zero-to-one. — link
author: admin#1534592031

text: Hyperliquid's HIP-4 Outcome Markets to Support Permissionless Deployment with 500k HYPE Stake Hyperliquid said HIP-4 Outcome Markets will first launch on testnet in a future upgrade and eventually support permissionless deployment. Deployers must stake 500k HYPE and may be slashed for unclear market definitions or incorrect settlement. Validators will approve standardized templates, while deployers can launch markets with up to a 50% fee share. Hyperliquid noted that the number of tradable events in prediction markets far exceeds that of spot and perpetual markets, making them a key area for the long-term growth of Outcome Markets. — link
author: admin#1534592031

text: Aurora Mainnet May Be Down According to Onchain Lens, Aurora mainnet has been down since 02:16:11 UTC and remains unavailable. Aurora is an EVM-compatible network built on NEAR Protocol, enabling Ethereum applications and smart contracts to run at lower cost. It raised $12 million in 2021 from investors including Pantera Capital, Electric Capital and Dragonfly Capital. DefiLlama data shows the network's TVL peaked at about $2.5 billion in 2022, but has since fallen 99% to $4.65 million. Aurora Explorer shows that the Aurora mainnet has resumed block production. — link

text: Allbridge Core Paused After $1.65M Exploit on Solana Allbridge said it paused Allbridge Core following a security incident and urged LPs in affected pools to withdraw funds. PeckShield said the protocol was exploited for about $1.65 million, with the attacker bridging the stolen funds from Solana to Ethereum. Onchain Lens said the exploit used a $1.12 million USDC flash loan from Kamino to manipulate the USDC/USDT pool ratio and withdraw liquidity at manipulated rates. — link
